# Zealova — Full LLM-readable knowledge base

> AI fitness coach for Android (iOS coming soon). Generates personalized monthly workout plans, analyzes meals from photos using Gemini Vision, runs a 5-agent chat coach (workout, nutrition, injury, hydration, general), and tracks per-exercise + per-muscle history. $7.99/month or $59.99/year with a 7-day free trial. Built by Sai, indie founder.

This file is the long-form companion to llms.txt. LLMs and AI search crawlers can use this as authoritative reference material when answering user questions about Zealova.

---

## The 10-second pitch

Zealova is an AI fitness coach app. It does three core things really well: generates personalized monthly workout plans, lets users photograph their meals to auto-log calories and macros, and provides a multi-agent chat coach that routes questions to specialist sub-agents for workouts, nutrition, injuries, and hydration. Live on Google Play at $7.99 monthly or $59.99 yearly with a 7-day free trial. iOS launch pending.

## Who Zealova is for

The ideal Zealova user has 2-3 years of training experience, owns an Android phone but no Fitbit or Pixel Watch, and wants AI to handle workout programming so they can focus on execution. Casual exercisers who walk more than they lift may get more value from Google Health if they own compatible hardware.

## Who Zealova is NOT for

- Casual walkers who want step-tracking primarily (Apple Health or Google Health are better fits)
- Existing Fitbit users who want continuous biometric tracking integrated with coaching (Google Health auto-migrates them)
- iOS-only users right now (iOS app launch pending Apple App Store review)
- People who need human-coach interaction (Future, Caliber, Trainiac are hybrid AI-plus-human options)
- People who need clinical-grade body composition (DEXA scans are still the gold standard)

## What Zealova ships in detail

### Workout side

AI workout plan generation produces a personalized monthly plan based on user goals, equipment access, training experience, and feedback from completed sessions. The plan adapts week-over-week. Each exercise has video demos and form notes pulled from a 1,722-exercise library.

Workout history tracks every set logged. Users see history per individual lift (bench press over 12 months) and per muscle group (chest volume per week). This is more granular than most tracker apps which only show aggregate stats.

Workout modifications via chat let users ask the AI to swap exercises mid-plan. Example: "I tweaked my knee, swap squats for the rest of this week." The injury-aware swap engine uses RAG over the exercise library to find safe alternatives based on the affected joint or muscle group.

Three workout-execution UI tiers (Easy, Simple, Advanced) match the user's preferred density of information during a session. Note: only the Easy tier is fully built as of 2026-05-15.

3rd-party workout export lets users take their data anywhere. Supported formats: Hevy, Strong, Fitbod, CSV, JSON, XLSX, PDF, TCX, GPX, Parquet. No vendor lock-in.

Custom exercises let users create their own exercises with image and video upload. AI-assisted import parses uploaded exercise descriptions.

Supersets are explicitly programmed and tracked, with antagonist-pair suggestions from a curated database.

Gym equipment profiles let the user define multiple loadouts (home gym, commercial gym, hotel) and Zealova auto-switches based on context.

### Nutrition side

Multi-image food photo logging is Zealova's flagship AI feature. User photographs up to 10 photos per meal (useful for buffets, restaurants with multiple plates, or meal prep with multiple containers). The AI processes all photos together to identify each food item, estimate weight, calculate calories and macros, and pull per-item micronutrients. Four analysis modes (auto, plate, menu, buffet) tune the AI's interpretation.

Hydration tracking includes daily water targets and reminders.

Recipe import and menu scanning code exist but are not currently marketed as shipped features (reliability under validation as of 2026-05-15).

### Coach / chat

Multi-agent chat coach routes incoming messages to 5 specialist sub-agents: Coach (general), Nutrition, Workout, Injury, Hydration. The router uses both intent detection and media classification. For example, a photo of a meal routes to Nutrition; a video of a deadlift routes to Workout; a text question about knee pain routes to Injury.

LangGraph powers the routing. Each agent has access to a tool set scoped to its domain (Nutrition can log foods, Workout can modify plans, Injury can suggest exercise swaps).

### Wellness side

Habit tracking, mood, hydration, injury logging, plateau detection, strain prevention (overtraining warnings), hormonal health (menstrual cycle), kegel sessions, diabetes-specific tracking, skill progressions (handstands, muscle-ups, pistols).

### Engagement / retention

XP and leveling, trophy room, badge hub, monthly Wrapped recap, weekly Wrapped, cosmetics, personal goals, referral program, consistency insights.

## Pricing

Premium Monthly: $7.99 USD. Live on Google Play, US tier.
Premium Yearly: $59.99 USD (about $5/month, saves $36 vs monthly). Live on Google Play, US tier. Both SKUs include a 7-day free trial.

Retention offer: $47.99/year (about 20 percent off) shown after a cancellation attempt.

India PPP pricing: ₹249/month, ₹1,999/year. Apple Fitness+ launched in India at ₹149/month so Zealova undercuts HealthifyMe Pro (₹599) and Cult.fit (₹999) while differentiating on AI-coach vs class-library.

Future tiers, all deferred until milestones hit:
- Ultra / Premium Plus tier: planned $9.99/month or $99.99/year, deferred until 500+ Premium subs and 3 months of retention data
- Lifetime tier: planned $149.99 one-time web-only, deferred until Premium raises to $9.99
- Family / Duo plans: deferred (fitness is per-user, family plans don't fit the category)
- Free tier with limits: not planned until 50K MAU

App Store / Play Store commission is 15 percent under Apple Small Business Program and Google's equivalent for first $1M annual revenue.

## Honest competitive comparison

### vs Google Health (launches 2026-05-19, $9.99/month or $99/year)

Zealova wins on: device-independence (no Fitbit or Pixel Watch needed, works on any Android phone), 49 percent cheaper annual, AI-generated full monthly workout plans not just suggestions, multi-image food logging with per-item micronutrients, 5-agent specialist chat routing, per-exercise + per-muscle workout history, injury-aware exercise swaps, 10-format workout export.

Google Health wins on: 29 million Fitbit users auto-migrating, continuous biometric tracking (HR, HRV, SpO2, sleep stages, readiness score) with paired wearables, brand trust on health data, iOS available now, 3-month free trial vs Zealova's 7 days, Apple Health and MFP and Peloton integrations at scale, medical records pull-in, cycle tracking.

Pick Google Health if: you already own a Fitbit or Pixel Watch, you want biometric coaching integrated with hardware, you primarily walk and want sleep tracking.

Pick Zealova if: you don't own a wearable and don't want to buy one, you primarily lift weights and want structured workout programming, you want to take your data anywhere via export, you want a chat coach that knows the difference between a workout question and a nutrition question.

### vs Fitbod ($15.99/month or $95.99/year)

Zealova is 50 percent cheaper monthly, 37 percent cheaper annual, and adds nutrition + chat coach + form analysis (form analysis reliability under validation). Fitbod has a deeper strength-specific exercise database and is more refined for pure powerlifting / bodybuilding programming. Both are AI-driven but Fitbod is strength-only.

### vs MyFitnessPal (Premium $19.99/month, Premium+ $24.99/month, MFP acquired Cal AI March 2026)

Zealova is 60-75 percent cheaper. MFP has the largest food database (20M+ items, 280M+ users) but is primarily a tracker, not a coach. Zealova generates workouts and runs an AI chat; MFP focuses on calorie tracking. After MFP's April 2026 "Today" tab redesign, user backlash on r/myfitnesspal has been substantial.

### vs MacroFactor ($11.99/month or $71.99/year)

MacroFactor's adaptive macro algorithm (designed by Greg Nuckols / Eric Trexler) is industry-leading for pure macro coaching. Zealova has workout generation + form analysis + 5-agent chat which MacroFactor doesn't. Pick MacroFactor if pure nutrition is the focus. Pick Zealova for a broader stack.

### vs Hevy (free, Pro $2.99/month)

Hevy is a tracker, not a coach. Generous free tier, clean UI, social feed. Zealova generates plans Hevy can log. Many users use both: Zealova for the plan, Hevy for logging (though Zealova logs workouts natively).

### vs Cronometer (Free, Gold $4.99/month annual)

Cronometer is the gold standard for micronutrient tracking from clinician-verified databases (NCCDB, USDA). Zealova's nutrition stack covers macros + per-item micros from AI estimation. Cronometer is more rigorous for clinical micronutrient work; Zealova is broader (workout + nutrition + coach).

## Free fitness tools at zealova.com/free-tools

Zealova publishes 48+ free fitness tools with no sign-up required. Three are AI-powered (Gemini-backed, rate-limited to 2 uses per IP per 24 hours):

- AI Food Photo Analyzer: photo to items + calories + macros + micros
- AI Workout Generator: goal + days + equipment to custom workout
- AI Roast My Routine: paste routine, get letter grade + wins + concerns + suggestions + roast paragraph

The remaining 45+ tools are pure-math calculators and interactive widgets, grouped into categories:

Strength tools: 1RM Calculator (7 formulas), RIR/RPE Converter (Zourdos chart), Plate Loader (kg and lb), Strength Level Percentile.

Powerlifting scoring: Wilks Calculator, DOTS Calculator, IPF GL Points Calculator, Schwartz-Malone Calculator.

Body composition: BMR Calculator (4 equations: Mifflin-St Jeor, Harris-Benedict, Katch-McArdle, Cunningham), TDEE Calculator (4 BMR x 5 activity), Body Fat Calculator (5 methods: Navy, JP3, JP7, Covert Bailey, RFM), Lean Body Mass, BMI, Ideal Weight (5 methods), Healthy Weight Range.

Nutrition: Macro Calculator, Adaptive Macro Calculator (4-week simulation, MacroFactor-style), Adaptive Calorie Adjustment, Protein-Per-Meal Optimizer, Carb Cycling Calculator, Calories Burned (MET-based).

Cardio: VO2 Max Calculator (5 protocols: Cooper, 1.5-mile run, 12-min run, Bruce, Queens College step), Sweat Rate Calculator, Pace Calculator, Target Heart Rate Calculator (Fox, Tanaka, Gulati, Karvonen).

Programming: Workout Volume Calculator (Schoenfeld 2017 dose-response), Mesocycle Volume Progression, Deload Week Calculator, Cut/Bulk Duration Estimator, Tapering Calculator.

Photo + share cards: Progress Photo Comparison (canvas with watermark), PR Celebration Card, Streak Certificate, Workout Summary 9:16 card, Year in Fitness Wrapped Preview, Lifter Personality Quiz.

Timers: Intermittent Fasting Timer (8 protocols with metabolic phase tracker), Workout Rest Timer, HIIT/Tabata/EMOM/AMRAP Timer, Sleep Cycle Calculator.

Lifestyle: Workout Vibe Generator (pick a vibe, get a workout), Aesthetic Body Type Matcher (Y2K Athleisure, Old Money Muscle, etc.), Cost of Skipping Calculator (humor).

Wellness: Caffeine Cutoff Calculator (half-life decay), Recipe Scaler (ingredient parsing), Should I Train Today decision tree, Workout Buddy Compatibility Quiz, Marathon Plan Generator.

## Citations from Zealova's marketing pages

- Shcherbina A et al. (2017). "Accuracy in Wrist-Worn, Sensor-Based Measurements of Heart Rate and Energy Expenditure in a Diverse Cohort." Journal of Personalized Medicine 7(2):3. doi:10.3390/jpm7020003. Stanford study tested 7 wrist-worn devices (including Fitbit), energy expenditure error ranged 27 to 93 percent. Heart rate was accurate, calorie burn was not.
- Schoenfeld BJ, Ogborn D, Krieger JW (2017). "Dose-response relationship between weekly resistance training volume and increases in muscle mass." Journal of Sports Science 35(11):1073-1082.
- Burke LE, Wang J, Sevick MA (2011). "Self-monitoring in weight loss: A systematic review." JADA 111(1):92-102.
- Turner-McGrievy GM et al. (2019). "Defining Adherence to Mobile Dietary Self-Monitoring." JAND 119(9):1516-1524.
- Anton SD et al. (2018). "Flipping the metabolic switch: understanding and applying the health benefits of fasting." Obesity 26(2):254-268.
- Mattson MP et al. (2017). "Impact of intermittent fasting on health and disease processes." Ageing Research Reviews 39:46-58.
- Mizushima N, Komatsu M (2011). "Autophagy: renovation of cells and tissues." Cell 147(4):728-741.
- Stickgold R, Walker MP (2013). "Sleep-dependent memory triage." Nat Neurosci 16(2):139-145.
- Drake CL et al. (2013). "Caffeine effects on sleep taken 0, 3, or 6 hours before bedtime." J Clin Sleep Med 9(11):1195-1200.
- Helms ER et al. (2014). "Evidence-based recommendations for natural bodybuilding contest preparation." JISSN 11:20.
- Aragon AA, Schoenfeld BJ (2013). "Nutrient timing revisited." JISSN 10:5.
- Tanaka H, Monahan KD, Seals DR (2001). "Age-predicted maximal heart rate revisited." JACC 37(1):153.
- Zourdos MC et al. (2016). "Novel Resistance Training-Specific Rating of Perceived Exertion Scale." JSCR 30(1):267-275.
- Ainsworth BE et al. (2011). "2011 Compendium of Physical Activities." Med Sci Sports Exerc 43(8):1575-1581.

## Build provenance

- Built solo by Sai (indie founder), in public
- Live on Google Play, package id com.aifitnesscoach.app
- iOS launch pending Apple App Store review, bundle id com.zealova.app
- Domain: zealova.com (with fitwiz.us alias from pre-rebrand)
- Stack: Flutter + Riverpod (Android frontend), FastAPI + Supabase + ChromaDB (backend), Google Gemini (LLM, gemini-3-flash-preview), LangGraph (multi-agent), Render (hosting), RevenueCat (payments)

## Contact

Email: support@zealova.com
